vulnerability.title: Configure Notification of Post-AIDE Scan Details
vulnerability.description: AIDE should notify appropriate personnel of the details of a scan after the scan has been run. If AIDE has already been configured for periodic execution in /etc/crontab, append the following line to the existing AIDE line: | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost Otherwise, add the following line to /etc/crontab: 05 4 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost AIDE can be executed periodically through other means; this is merely one example.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "aide" ; then dnf install -y "aide" fi var_aide_scan_notification_email='' CRONTAB=/etc/crontab CRONDIRS='/etc/cron.d /etc/cron.daily /etc/cron.weekly /etc/cron.monthly' # NOTE: on some platforms, /etc/crontab may not exist if [ -f /etc/crontab ]; then CRONTAB_EXIST=/etc/crontab fi if [ -f /var/spool/cron/root ]; then VARSPOOL=/var/spool/cron/root fi if ! grep -qR '^.*/usr/sbin/aide\s*\-\-check.*|.*\/bin\/mail\s*-s\s*".*"\s*.*@.*$' $CRONTAB_EXIST $VARSPOOL $CRONDIRS; then echo "0 5 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s \"\$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check\" $var_aide_scan_notification_email" >> $CRONTAB fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done'

triage_decision.reason: AIDE should notify appropriate personnel of scan details after execution. Missing or misconfigured notification could lead to undetected issues.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: misconfiguration of AIDE cron job; lack of email alerting

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to output JSON with fields: plan_description, commands, etc. The plan description should contain rationale, files to modify, commands to run.

We have one finding: configure Notification of Post-AIDE Scan Details. Need to ensure AIDE will notify appropriate personnel of the details of a scan after the scan has been run.

Thus remediation: configure AIDE to send email after scan. Likely need to add a cron job that runs after AIDE scan? Or modify /etc/aide.conf to set email alerts. Let's think: The finding ID is aide_scan_notification. We need to ensure that AIDE notifies via email. So we can edit /etc/aide.conf to set email address.

Plan description: "Configure AIDE to send email notifications for scan results by editing /etc/aide.conf and adding a cron job to trigger email after scan."

Commands: we need to run some commands: maybe modify /etc/aide.conf using sed, then restart aide service, maybe enable and start cron.

We need to use only the allowed tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file.

We can read the file to see current config, then write_file to modify.

Thus steps:

1. read_file /etc/aide.conf (to see current config)
2. write_file /etc/aide.conf with new content (e.g., set email = "admin@example.com")
3. run_cmd systemctl restart aide (or service aide restart)
4. run_cmd crontab -l to list current cron? maybe add a cron job: we can write_file to /etc/cron.d/aide_notify with a line like "@reboot /usr/sbin/aide --check --email". However we don't know exact path. We'll just add a cron entry in /etc/cron.d/aide_notify.

We need to ensure we use only allowed tools. So we can use write_file to create /etc/cron.d/aide_notify.

Thus final JSON:

{
 "plan_description": "Configure AIDE to email scan results by setting email address in /etc/aide.conf and adding a cron job to trigger email after scan.",
 "commands": [
   {"cmd": "read_file /etc/aide.conf"},
   {"cmd": "write_file /etc/aide.conf email = \"secops@example.com\""},
   {"cmd": "run_cmd systemctl restart aide"},
   {"cmd": "write_file /etc/cron.d/aide_notify @reboot /usr/sbin/aide --check --email secops@example.com"},
   {"cmd": "run_cmd chmod 644 /etc/cron.d/aide_notify"}
 ]
}

But need to ensure format: each command likely just string. We'll provide as array of strings.

Let's craft final answer.

We need to produce JSON with plan_description, etc. Provide commands to fix: need to configure AIDE to send email notifications of scan details.

Steps: Ensure AIDE installed, configure /etc/aide.conf to set email and maybe add a cron job to run aide --check and mail output.

We'll produce a plan with commands: install aide if not present, edit /etc/aide.conf to set email address, add a cron entry.

Let's craft JSON.
